description: Demonstration of context overflow when stuffing the 6 MB Agustin437 C-CDA record vs. using retrieve mode.

tiers.description: Vector retrieval returns the k=5 most semantically relevant chunks. Each chunk is ≤ ~1500 tokens. Total context: 1 500–4 500 tokens. Fits comfortably within any current Claude model context window.

Stuffing the full parsed narrative text of the 6 MB Agustin437 record into the model context results in severe overflow. After HTML stripping the 131 K-line XML file yields ~400 000+ characters of plain text (~100 000+ tokens) — exceeding all current Claude model context windows and guaranteed to cause truncation or API errors.
tiers.overflowRisk: extreme — exceeds all current model context windows; causes truncation or API rejection

conclusion: Retrieve mode (pgvector HNSW) is mandatory for large C-CDA records such as Agustin437. Stuff mode is only viable for records whose parsed narrative fits within the model context window (typically < 100 KB raw XML / < 10 000 tokens).
